* * *

`Qlib` Documentation[](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/latest/#qlib-documentation "Permalink to this heading")

===============================================================================================================

`Qlib` is an AI-oriented quantitative investment platform, which aims to realize the potential, empower the research, and create the value of AI technologies in quantitative investment.

Document Structure[](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/latest/#document-structure "Permalink to this heading")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

GETTING STARTED:

*   [Introduction](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/latest/introduction/introduction.html)

    *   [Framework](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/latest/introduction/introduction.html#framework)
        
*   [Quick Start](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/latest/introduction/quick.html)

    *   [Auto Quant Research Workflow](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/latest/introduction/quick.html#auto-quant-research-workflow)

        *   [1\. RuntimeError: An attempt has been made to start a new process before the current process has finished its bootstrapping phase…](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/latest/FAQ/FAQ.html#runtimeerror-an-attempt-has-been-made-to-start-a-new-process-before-the-current-process-has-finished-its-bootstrapping-phase)
            
        *   [2\. qlib.data.cache.QlibCacheException: It sees the key(…) of the redis lock has existed in your redis db now.](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/latest/FAQ/FAQ.html#qlib-data-cache-qlibcacheexception-it-sees-the-key-of-the-redis-lock-has-existed-in-your-redis-db-now)
            
        *   [3\. ModuleNotFoundError: No module named ‘qlib.data.\_libs.rolling’](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/latest/FAQ/FAQ.html#modulenotfounderror-no-module-named-qlib-data-libs-rolling)
            
        *   [4\. BadNamespaceError: / is not a connected namespace](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/latest/FAQ/FAQ.html#badnamespaceerror-is-not-a-connected-namespace)
            
        *   [5\. TypeError: send() got an unexpected keyword argument ‘binary’](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/latest/FAQ/FAQ.html#typeerror-send-got-an-unexpected-keyword-argument-binary)

# Qlib Documentation — QLib 0.9.7 documentation

*   [](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/v0.9.7/#)
    
*   `Qlib` Documentation
*   [View page source](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/v0.9.7/_sources/index.rst.txt)
    

* * *

`Qlib` Documentation[](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/v0.9.7/#qlib-documentation "Permalink to this heading")

===============================================================================================================

`Qlib` is an AI-oriented quantitative investment platform, which aims to realize the potential, empower the research, and create the value of AI technologies in quantitative investment.

Document Structure[](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/v0.9.7/#document-structure "Permalink to this heading")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

RuntimeError: An attempt has been made to start a new process before the current process has finished its bootstrapping phase…](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/v0.9.7/FAQ/FAQ.html#runtimeerror-an-attempt-has-been-made-to-start-a-new-process-before-the-current-process-has-finished-its-bootstrapping-phase)

        *   [3\. ModuleNotFoundError: No module named ‘qlib.data.\_libs.rolling’](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/v0.9.7/FAQ/FAQ.html#modulenotfounderror-no-module-named-qlib-data-libs-rolling)
            
        *   [4\. BadNamespaceError: / is not a connected namespace](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/v0.9.7/FAQ/FAQ.html#badnamespaceerror-is-not-a-connected-namespace)

# Qlib Documentation — QLib 0.9.7 documentation

*   [](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/stable/#)
    
*   `Qlib` Documentation
*   [View page source](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/stable/_sources/index.rst.txt)
    

* * *

`Qlib` Documentation[](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/stable/#qlib-documentation "Permalink to this heading")

===============================================================================================================

`Qlib` is an AI-oriented quantitative investment platform, which aims to realize the potential, empower the research, and create the value of AI technologies in quantitative investment.

Document Structure[](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/stable/#document-structure "Permalink to this heading")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

RuntimeError: An attempt has been made to start a new process before the current process has finished its bootstrapping phase…](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/stable/FAQ/FAQ.html#runtimeerror-an-attempt-has-been-made-to-start-a-new-process-before-the-current-process-has-finished-its-bootstrapping-phase)

        *   [3\. ModuleNotFoundError: No module named ‘qlib.data.\_libs.rolling’](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/stable/FAQ/FAQ.html#modulenotfounderror-no-module-named-qlib-data-libs-rolling)

# Qlib Documentation — QLib 0.9.6 documentation

*   [](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/v0.9.6/#)
    
*   `Qlib` Documentation
*   [View page source](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/v0.9.6/_sources/index.rst.txt)
    

* * *

`Qlib` Documentation[](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/v0.9.6/#qlib-documentation "Permalink to this heading")

===============================================================================================================

`Qlib` is an AI-oriented quantitative investment platform, which aims to realize the potential, empower the research, and create the value of AI technologies in quantitative investment.

Document Structure[](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/v0.9.6/#document-structure "Permalink to this heading")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

RuntimeError: An attempt has been made to start a new process before the current process has finished its bootstrapping phase…](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/v0.9.6/FAQ/FAQ.html#runtimeerror-an-attempt-has-been-made-to-start-a-new-process-before-the-current-process-has-finished-its-bootstrapping-phase)

        *   [3\. ModuleNotFoundError: No module named ‘qlib.data.\_libs.rolling’](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/v0.9.6/FAQ/FAQ.html#modulenotfounderror-no-module-named-qlib-data-libs-rolling)
            
        *   [4\. BadNamespaceError: / is not a connected namespace](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/v0.9.6/FAQ/FAQ.html#badnamespaceerror-is-not-a-connected-namespace)
            
        *   [5\. TypeError: send() got an unexpected keyword argument ‘binary’](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/v0.9.6/FAQ/FAQ.html#typeerror-send-got-an-unexpected-keyword-argument-binary)

# Qlib Documentation — QLib 0.9.2 documentation

*   [Docs](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/v0.9.2/#)
     »
*   `Qlib` Documentation
*   [Edit on GitHub](https://github.com/microsoft/qlib/blob/v0.9.2/docs/index.rst)
    

* * *

`Qlib` Documentation[¶](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/v0.9.2/#qlib-documentation "Permalink to this headline")

================================================================================================================

`Qlib` is an AI-oriented quantitative investment platform, which aims to realize the potential, empower the research, and create the value of AI technologies in quantitative investment.

Document Structure[¶](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/v0.9.2/#document-structure "Permalink to this headline")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

        *   [1\. RuntimeError: An attempt has been made to start a new process before the current process has finished its bootstrapping phase…](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/v0.9.2/FAQ/FAQ.html#runtimeerror-an-attempt-has-been-made-to-start-a-new-process-before-the-current-process-has-finished-its-bootstrapping-phase)
            
        *   [2\. qlib.data.cache.QlibCacheException: It sees the key(…) of the redis lock has existed in your redis db now.](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/v0.9.2/FAQ/FAQ.html#qlib-data-cache-qlibcacheexception-it-sees-the-key-of-the-redis-lock-has-existed-in-your-redis-db-now)
            
        *   [3\. ModuleNotFoundError: No module named ‘qlib.data.\_libs.rolling’](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/v0.9.2/FAQ/FAQ.html#modulenotfounderror-no-module-named-qlib-data-libs-rolling)
            
        *   [4\. BadNamespaceError: / is not a connected namespace](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/v0.9.2/FAQ/FAQ.html#badnamespaceerror-is-not-a-connected-namespace)
            
        *   [5\. TypeError: send() got an unexpected keyword argument ‘binary’](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/v0.9.2/FAQ/FAQ.html#typeerror-send-got-an-unexpected-keyword-argument-binary)

# Qlib Documentation — QLib 0.9.5 documentation

*   [](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/v0.9.5/#)
    
*   `Qlib` Documentation
*   [Edit on GitHub](https://github.com/microsoft/qlib/blob/v0.9.5/docs/index.rst)
    

* * *

`Qlib` Documentation[](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/v0.9.5/#qlib-documentation "Permalink to this heading")

===============================================================================================================

`Qlib` is an AI-oriented quantitative investment platform, which aims to realize the potential, empower the research, and create the value of AI technologies in quantitative investment.

Document Structure[](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/v0.9.5/#document-structure "Permalink to this heading")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Qlib Documentation — QLib 0.9.1 documentation

*   [Docs](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/v0.9.1/#)
     »
*   `Qlib` Documentation
*   [Edit on GitHub](https://github.com/microsoft/qlib/blob/v0.9.1/docs/index.rst)
    

* * *

`Qlib` Documentation[¶](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/v0.9.1/#qlib-documentation "Permalink to this headline")

================================================================================================================

`Qlib` is an AI-oriented quantitative investment platform, which aims to realize the potential, empower the research, and create the value of AI technologies in quantitative investment.

Document Structure[¶](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/v0.9.1/#document-structure "Permalink to this headline")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

RuntimeError: An attempt has been made to start a new process before the current process has finished its bootstrapping phase…](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/v0.9.1/FAQ/FAQ.html#runtimeerror-an-attempt-has-been-made-to-start-a-new-process-before-the-current-process-has-finished-its-bootstrapping-phase)
            
        *   [2\. qlib.data.cache.QlibCacheException: It sees the key(…) of the redis lock has existed in your redis db now.](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/v0.9.1/FAQ/FAQ.html#qlib-data-cache-qlibcacheexception-it-sees-the-key-of-the-redis-lock-has-existed-in-your-redis-db-now)
            
        *   [3\. ModuleNotFoundError: No module named ‘qlib.data.\_libs.rolling’](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/v0.9.1/FAQ/FAQ.html#modulenotfounderror-no-module-named-qlib-data-libs-rolling)
            
        *   [4\. BadNamespaceError: / is not a connected namespace](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/v0.9.1/FAQ/FAQ.html#badnamespaceerror-is-not-a-connected-namespace)
            
        *   [5\. TypeError: send() got an unexpected keyword argument ‘binary’](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/v0.9.1/FAQ/FAQ.html#typeerror-send-got-an-unexpected-keyword-argument-binary)

# Qlib Documentation — QLib 0.9.0 documentation

*   [Docs](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/v0.9.0/#)
     »
*   `Qlib` Documentation
*   [Edit on GitHub](https://github.com/microsoft/qlib/blob/v0.9.0/docs/index.rst)
    

* * *

`Qlib` Documentation[¶](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/v0.9.0/#qlib-documentation "Permalink to this headline")

================================================================================================================

`Qlib` is an AI-oriented quantitative investment platform, which aims to realize the potential, empower the research, and create the value of AI technologies in quantitative investment.

Document Structure[¶](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/v0.9.0/#document-structure "Permalink to this headline")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

RuntimeError: An attempt has been made to start a new process before the current process has finished its bootstrapping phase…](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/v0.9.0/FAQ/FAQ.html#runtimeerror-an-attempt-has-been-made-to-start-a-new-process-before-the-current-process-has-finished-its-bootstrapping-phase)
            
        *   [2\. qlib.data.cache.QlibCacheException: It sees the key(…) of the redis lock has existed in your redis db now.](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/v0.9.0/FAQ/FAQ.html#qlib-data-cache-qlibcacheexception-it-sees-the-key-of-the-redis-lock-has-existed-in-your-redis-db-now)
            
        *   [3\. ModuleNotFoundError: No module named ‘qlib.data.\_libs.rolling’](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/v0.9.0/FAQ/FAQ.html#modulenotfounderror-no-module-named-qlib-data-libs-rolling)
            
        *   [4\. BadNamespaceError: / is not a connected namespace](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/v0.9.0/FAQ/FAQ.html#badnamespaceerror-is-not-a-connected-namespace)
            
        *   [5\. TypeError: send() got an unexpected keyword argument ‘binary’](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/v0.9.0/FAQ/FAQ.html#typeerror-send-got-an-unexpected-keyword-argument-binary)

# Qlib Documentation — QLib 0.8.6 documentation

*   [Docs](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/v0.8.6/#)
     »
*   `Qlib` Documentation
*   [Edit on GitHub](https://github.com/microsoft/qlib/blob/v0.8.6/docs/index.rst)
    

* * *

`Qlib` Documentation[¶](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/v0.8.6/#qlib-documentation "Permalink to this headline")

================================================================================================================

`Qlib` is an AI-oriented quantitative investment platform, which aims to realize the potential, empower the research, and create the value of AI technologies in quantitative investment.

Document Structure[¶](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/v0.8.6/#document-structure "Permalink to this headline")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Qlib Documentation — QLib 0.8.4.99 documentation

*   [Docs](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/v0.8.5/#)
     »
*   `Qlib` Documentation
*   [Edit on GitHub](https://github.com/microsoft/qlib/blob/v0.8.5/docs/index.rst)
    

* * *

`Qlib` Documentation[¶](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/v0.8.5/#qlib-documentation "Permalink to this headline")

================================================================================================================

`Qlib` is an AI-oriented quantitative investment platform, which aims to realize the potential, empower the research, and create the value of AI technologies in quantitative investment.

Document Structure[¶](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/v0.8.5/#document-structure "Permalink to this headline")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Qlib Documentation — QLib 0.8.3 documentation

*   [Docs](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/v0.8.3/#)
     »
*   `Qlib` Documentation
*   [Edit on GitHub](https://github.com/microsoft/qlib/blob/v0.8.3/docs/index.rst)
    

* * *

`Qlib` Documentation[¶](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/v0.8.3/#qlib-documentation "Permalink to this headline")

================================================================================================================

`Qlib` is an AI-oriented quantitative investment platform, which aims to realize the potential, empower the research, and create the value of AI technologies in quantitative investment.

Document Structure[¶](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/v0.8.3/#document-structure "Permalink to this headline")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Qlib Documentation — QLib 0.6.2 documentation

*   [Docs](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/v0.6.3/#)
     »
*   `Qlib` Documentation
*   [Edit on GitHub](https://github.com/microsoft/qlib/blob/v0.6.3/docs/index.rst)
    

* * *

`Qlib` Documentation[¶](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/v0.6.3/#qlib-documentation "Permalink to this headline")

================================================================================================================

`Qlib` is an AI-oriented quantitative investment platform, which aims to realize the potential, empower the research, and create the value of AI technologies in quantitative investment.

Document Structure[¶](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/v0.6.3/#document-structure "Permalink to this headline")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

GETTING STARTED:

*   [Introduction](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/v0.6.3/introduction/introduction.html)

    *   [Framework](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/v0.6.3/introduction/introduction.html#framework)
        
*   [Quick Start](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/v0.6.3/introduction/quick.html)

# Qlib Documentation — QLib 0.6.2 documentation

*   [Docs](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/v0.6.2/#)
     »
*   `Qlib` Documentation
*   [Edit on GitHub](https://github.com/microsoft/qlib/blob/v0.6.2/docs/index.rst)
    

* * *

`Qlib` Documentation[¶](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/v0.6.2/#qlib-documentation "Permalink to this headline")

================================================================================================================

`Qlib` is an AI-oriented quantitative investment platform, which aims to realize the potential, empower the research, and create the value of AI technologies in quantitative investment.

Document Structure[¶](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/v0.6.2/#document-structure "Permalink to this headline")

# Qlib Documentation — QLib 0.7.2 documentation

*   [Docs](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.2/#)
     »
*   `Qlib` Documentation
*   [Edit on GitHub](https://github.com/microsoft/qlib/blob/v0.7.2/docs/index.rst)
    

* * *

`Qlib` Documentation[¶](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.2/#qlib-documentation "Permalink to this headline")

================================================================================================================

`Qlib` is an AI-oriented quantitative investment platform, which aims to realize the potential, empower the research, and create the value of AI technologies in quantitative investment.

Document Structure[¶](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.2/#document-structure "Permalink to this headline")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Qlib Documentation — QLib 0.9.3 documentation

*   [Docs](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/v0.9.3/#)
     »
*   `Qlib` Documentation
*   [Edit on GitHub](https://github.com/microsoft/qlib/blob/v0.9.3/docs/index.rst)
    

* * *

`Qlib` Documentation[¶](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/v0.9.3/#qlib-documentation "Permalink to this headline")

================================================================================================================

`Qlib` is an AI-oriented quantitative investment platform, which aims to realize the potential, empower the research, and create the value of AI technologies in quantitative investment.

Document Structure[¶](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/v0.9.3/#document-structure "Permalink to this headline")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

RuntimeError: An attempt has been made to start a new process before the current process has finished its bootstrapping phase…](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/v0.9.3/FAQ/FAQ.html#runtimeerror-an-attempt-has-been-made-to-start-a-new-process-before-the-current-process-has-finished-its-bootstrapping-phase)
            
        *   [2\. qlib.data.cache.QlibCacheException: It sees the key(…) of the redis lock has existed in your redis db now.](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/v0.9.3/FAQ/FAQ.html#qlib-data-cache-qlibcacheexception-it-sees-the-key-of-the-redis-lock-has-existed-in-your-redis-db-now)
            
        *   [3\. ModuleNotFoundError: No module named ‘qlib.data.\_libs.rolling’](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/v0.9.3/FAQ/FAQ.html#modulenotfounderror-no-module-named-qlib-data-libs-rolling)
            
        *   [4\. BadNamespaceError: / is not a connected namespace](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/v0.9.3/FAQ/FAQ.html#badnamespaceerror-is-not-a-connected-namespace)
            
        *   [5\. TypeError: send() got an unexpected keyword argument ‘binary’](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/v0.9.3/FAQ/FAQ.html#typeerror-send-got-an-unexpected-keyword-argument-binary)

# Qlib Documentation — QLib 0.8.0.99 documentation

*   [Docs](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/v0.8.1/#)
     »
*   `Qlib` Documentation
*   [Edit on GitHub](https://github.com/microsoft/qlib/blob/v0.8.1/docs/index.rst)
    

* * *

`Qlib` Documentation[¶](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/v0.8.1/#qlib-documentation "Permalink to this headline")

================================================================================================================

`Qlib` is an AI-oriented quantitative investment platform, which aims to realize the potential, empower the research, and create the value of AI technologies in quantitative investment.

Document Structure[¶](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/v0.8.1/#document-structure "Permalink to this headline")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

RuntimeError: An attempt has been made to start a new process before the current process has finished its bootstrapping phase…](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/v0.8.1/FAQ/FAQ.html#runtimeerror-an-attempt-has-been-made-to-start-a-new-process-before-the-current-process-has-finished-its-bootstrapping-phase)
            
        *   [2\. qlib.data.cache.QlibCacheException: It sees the key(…) of the redis lock has existed in your redis db now.](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/v0.8.1/FAQ/FAQ.html#qlib-data-cache-qlibcacheexception-it-sees-the-key-of-the-redis-lock-has-existed-in-your-redis-db-now)
            
        *   [3\. ModuleNotFoundError: No module named ‘qlib.data.\_libs.rolling’](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/v0.8.1/FAQ/FAQ.html#modulenotfounderror-no-module-named-qlib-data-libs-rolling)
            
        *   [4\. BadNamespaceError: / is not a connected namespace](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/v0.8.1/FAQ/FAQ.html#badnamespaceerror-is-not-a-connected-namespace)
            
        *   [5\. TypeError: send() got an unexpected keyword argument ‘binary’](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/v0.8.1/FAQ/FAQ.html#typeerror-send-got-an-unexpected-keyword-argument-binary)

# Qlib Documentation — QLib 0.4.6.dev0 documentation

*   [Docs](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/v0.5.0/#)
     »
*   `Qlib` Documentation
*   [Edit on GitHub](https://github.com/microsoft/qlib/blob/v0.5.0/docs/index.rst)
    

* * *

`Qlib` Documentation[¶](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/v0.5.0/#qlib-documentation "Permalink to this headline")

================================================================================================================

`Qlib` is an AI-oriented quantitative investment platform, which aims to realize the potential, empower the research, and create the value of AI technologies in quantitative investment.

Document Structure[¶](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/v0.5.0/#document-structure "Permalink to this headline")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Qlib Documentation — QLib 0.6.0 documentation

*   [Docs](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/v0.6.0/#)

* * *

`Qlib` Documentation[¶](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/v0.6.0/#qlib-documentation "Permalink to this headline")

================================================================================================================

`Qlib` is an AI-oriented quantitative investment platform, which aims to realize the potential, empower the research, and create the value of AI technologies in quantitative investment.

Document Structure[¶](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/v0.6.0/#document-structure "Permalink to this headline")

# Qlib Documentation — QLib 0.5.1 documentation

*   [Docs](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/v0.5.1/#)
     »
*   `Qlib` Documentation
*   [Edit on GitHub](https://github.com/microsoft/qlib/blob/v0.5.1/docs/index.rst)
    

* * *

`Qlib` Documentation[¶](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/v0.5.1/#qlib-documentation "Permalink to this headline")

================================================================================================================

`Qlib` is an AI-oriented quantitative investment platform, which aims to realize the potential, empower the research, and create the value of AI technologies in quantitative investment.

Document Structure[¶](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/v0.5.1/#document-structure "Permalink to this headline")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Qlib Documentation — QLib 0.6.1 documentation

*   [Docs](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/v0.6.1/#)

*   `Qlib` Documentation
*   [Edit on GitHub](https://github.com/microsoft/qlib/blob/v0.6.1/docs/index.rst)
    

* * *

`Qlib` Documentation[¶](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/v0.6.1/#qlib-documentation "Permalink to this headline")

================================================================================================================

`Qlib` is an AI-oriented quantitative investment platform, which aims to realize the potential, empower the research, and create the value of AI technologies in quantitative investment.

Document Structure[¶](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/v0.6.1/#document-structure "Permalink to this headline")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Qlib Documentation — QLib 0.8.4 documentation

*   [Docs](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/v0.8.4/#)
     »
*   `Qlib` Documentation
*   [Edit on GitHub](https://github.com/microsoft/qlib/blob/v0.8.4/docs/index.rst)
    

* * *

`Qlib` Documentation[¶](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/v0.8.4/#qlib-documentation "Permalink to this headline")

================================================================================================================

`Qlib` is an AI-oriented quantitative investment platform, which aims to realize the potential, empower the research, and create the value of AI technologies in quantitative investment.

Document Structure[¶](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/v0.8.4/#document-structure "Permalink to this headline")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Qlib Documentation — QLib 0.8.2 documentation

*   [Docs](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/v0.8.2/#)
     »
*   `Qlib` Documentation
*   [Edit on GitHub](https://github.com/microsoft/qlib/blob/v0.8.2/docs/index.rst)
    

* * *

`Qlib` Documentation[¶](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/v0.8.2/#qlib-documentation "Permalink to this headline")

================================================================================================================

`Qlib` is an AI-oriented quantitative investment platform, which aims to realize the potential, empower the research, and create the value of AI technologies in quantitative investment.

Document Structure[¶](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/v0.8.2/#document-structure "Permalink to this headline")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Qlib Documentation — QLib 0.8.0 documentation

*   [Docs](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/v0.8.0/#)
     »
*   `Qlib` Documentation
*   [Edit on GitHub](https://github.com/microsoft/qlib/blob/v0.8.0/docs/index.rst)

`Qlib` Documentation[¶](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/v0.8.0/#qlib-documentation "Permalink to this headline")

================================================================================================================

`Qlib` is an AI-oriented quantitative investment platform, which aims to realize the potential, empower the research, and create the value of AI technologies in quantitative investment.

Document Structure[¶](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/v0.8.0/#document-structure "Permalink to this headline")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

        *   [1\. RuntimeError: An attempt has been made to start a new process before the current process has finished its bootstrapping phase…](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/v0.8.0/FAQ/FAQ.html#runtimeerror-an-attempt-has-been-made-to-start-a-new-process-before-the-current-process-has-finished-its-bootstrapping-phase)
            
        *   [2\. qlib.data.cache.QlibCacheException: It sees the key(…) of the redis lock has existed in your redis db now.](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/v0.8.0/FAQ/FAQ.html#qlib-data-cache-qlibcacheexception-it-sees-the-key-of-the-redis-lock-has-existed-in-your-redis-db-now)
            
        *   [3\. ModuleNotFoundError: No module named ‘qlib.data.\_libs.rolling’](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/v0.8.0/FAQ/FAQ.html#modulenotfounderror-no-module-named-qlib-data-libs-rolling)
            
        *   [4\. BadNamespaceError: / is not a connected namespace](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/v0.8.0/FAQ/FAQ.html#badnamespaceerror-is-not-a-connected-namespace)
            
        *   [5\. TypeError: send() got an unexpected keyword argument ‘binary’](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/v0.8.0/FAQ/FAQ.html#typeerror-send-got-an-unexpected-keyword-argument-binary)

# Qlib: Quantitative Platform — QLib 0.9.8.dev11 documentation

*   [](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/latest/index.html)
    
*   `Qlib`: Quantitative Platform
*   [View page source](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/latest/_sources/introduction/introduction.rst.txt)
    

* * *

`Qlib`: Quantitative Platform[](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/latest/introduction/introduction.html#qlib-quantitative-platform "Permalink to this heading")

==============================================================================================================================================================

Introduction[](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/latest/introduction/introduction.html#introduction "Permalink to this heading")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

![../_images/white_bg_rec%2Bword.png](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/latest/_images/white_bg_rec%2Bword.png)

`Qlib` is an AI-oriented quantitative investment platform, which aims to realize the potential, empower the research, and create the value of AI technologies in quantitative investment.

With `Qlib`, users can easily try their ideas to create better Quant investment strategies.

Framework[](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/latest/introduction/introduction.html#framework "Permalink to this heading")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

![../_images/framework.svg](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/latest/_images/framework.svg)

At the module level, Qlib is a platform that consists of above components. The components are designed as loose-coupled modules and each component could be used stand-alone.

This framework may be intimidating for new users to Qlib. It tries to accurately include a lot of details of Qlib’s design. For users new to Qlib, you can skip it first and read it later.

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Infrastructure layer | Infrastructure layer provides underlying support for Quant research. DataServer provides high-performance infrastructure for users to manage and retrieve raw data. Trainer provides flexible interface to control the training process of models which enable algorithms controlling the training process. |
| Learning Framework layer | The Forecast Model and Trading Agent are trainable. They are trained based on the Learning Framework layer and then applied to multiple scenarios in Workflow layer. The supported learning paradigms can be categorized into reinforcement learning and supervised learning. The learning framework leverages the Workflow layer as well(e.g. sharing Information Extractor, creating environments based on Execution Env). |
| Workflow layer | Workflow layer covers the whole workflow of quantitative investment. Both supervised-learning-based strategies and RL-based Strategies are supported. Information Extractor extracts data for models. Forecast Model focuses on producing all kinds of forecast signals (e.g. _alpha_, risk) for other modules. With these signals Decision Generator will generate the target trading decisions(i.e. portfolio, orders) If RL-based Strategies are adopted, the Policy is learned in a end-to-end way, the trading decisions are generated directly. Decisions will be executed by Execution Env (i.e. the trading market). There may be multiple levels of Strategy and Executor (e.g. an _order executor trading strategy and intraday order executor_ could behave like an interday trading loop and be nested in _daily portfolio management trading strategy and interday trading executor_ trading loop) |
| Interface layer | Interface layer tries to present a user-friendly interface for the underlying system. Analyser module will provide users detailed analysis reports of forecasting signals, portfolios and execution results |

*   The modules with hand-drawn style are under development and will be released in the future.
    
*   The modules with dashed borders are highly user-customizable and extendible.
    

(p.s. framework image is created with [https://draw.io/](https://draw.io/)
)

---

# Installation — QLib 0.9.8.dev11 documentation

*   [](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/latest/index.html)
    
*   Installation
*   [View page source](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/latest/_sources/start/installation.rst.txt)
    

* * *

Installation[](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/latest/start/installation.html#installation "Permalink to this heading")

========================================================================================================================

`Qlib` Installation[](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/latest/start/installation.html#qlib-installation "Permalink to this heading")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Note

Qlib supports both Windows and Linux. It’s recommended to use Qlib in Linux. `Qlib` supports Python3, which is up to Python3.8.

Users can easily install `Qlib` by pip according to the following command:

pip install pyqlib

Also, Users can install `Qlib` by the source code according to the following steps:

*   Enter the root directory of `Qlib`, in which the file `setup.py` exists.
    
*   Then, please execute the following command to install the environment dependencies and install `Qlib`:
    
    > $ pip install numpy
    > $ pip install \--upgrade cython
    > $ git clone https://github.com/microsoft/qlib.git && cd qlib
    > $ python setup.py install
    

Note

It’s recommended to use anaconda/miniconda to setup the environment. `Qlib` needs lightgbm and pytorch packages, use pip to install them.

Use the following code to make sure the installation successful:

\>>> import qlib
\>>> qlib.\_\_version\_\_
<LATEST VERSION>

---

# Qlib Initialization — QLib 0.9.8.dev11 documentation

*   [](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/latest/index.html)
    
*   Qlib Initialization
*   [View page source](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/latest/_sources/start/initialization.rst.txt)
    

* * *

Qlib Initialization[](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/latest/start/initialization.html#qlib-initialization "Permalink to this heading")

========================================================================================================================================

Initialization[](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/latest/start/initialization.html#id1 "Permalink to this heading")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Please follow the steps below to initialize `Qlib`.

Download and prepare the Data: execute the following command to download stock data. Please pay attention that the data is collected from [Yahoo Finance](https://finance.yahoo.com/lookup)
 and the data might not be perfect. We recommend users to prepare their own data if they have high-quality datasets. Please refer to [Data](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/latest/component/data.html#converting-csv-format-into-qlib-format)
 for more information about customized dataset.

> python scripts/get\_data.py qlib\_data \--target\_dir ~/.qlib/qlib\_data/cn\_data \--region cn

Please refer to [Data Preparation](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/latest/component/data.html#data-preparation)
 for more information about get\_data.py,

Initialize Qlib before calling other APIs: run following code in python.

> import qlib
> \# region in \[REG\_CN, REG\_US\]
> from qlib.constant import REG\_CN
> provider\_uri \= "~/.qlib/qlib\_data/cn\_data"  \# target\_dir
> qlib.init(provider\_uri\=provider\_uri, region\=REG\_CN)

Note

Do not import qlib package in the repository directory of `Qlib`, otherwise, errors may occur.

### Parameters[](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/latest/start/initialization.html#parameters "Permalink to this heading")

Besides provider\_uri and region, qlib.init has other parameters. The following are several important parameters of qlib.init (Qlib has a lot of config. Only part of parameters are limited here. More detailed setting can be found [here](https://github.com/microsoft/qlib/blob/main/qlib/config.py)
):

*   provider\_uri
    
    Type: str. The URI of the Qlib data. For example, it could be the location where the data loaded by `get_data.py` are stored.
    
*   region
    
    Type: str, optional parameter(default: qlib.constant.REG\_CN).
    
    Currently: `qlib.constant.REG_US` (‘us’) and `qlib.constant.REG_CN` (‘cn’) is supported. Different value of region will result in different stock market mode. - `qlib.constant.REG_US`: US stock market. - `qlib.constant.REG_CN`: China stock market.
    
    Different modes will result in different trading limitations and costs. The region is just [shortcuts for defining a batch of configurations](https://github.com/microsoft/qlib/blob/528f74af099bf6156e9480bcd2bb28e453231212/qlib/config.py#L249)
    , which include minimal trading order unit (`trade_unit`), trading limitation (`limit_threshold`) , etc. It is not a necessary part and users can set the key configurations manually if the existing region setting can’t meet their requirements.
    
*   redis\_host
    
    Type: str, optional parameter(default: “127.0.0.1”), host of redis
    
    The lock and cache mechanism relies on redis.
    
*   redis\_port
    
    Type: int, optional parameter(default: 6379), port of redis
    
    Note
    
    The value of region should be aligned with the data stored in provider\_uri. Currently, `scripts/get_data.py` only provides China stock market data. If users want to use the US stock market data, they should prepare their own US-stock data in provider\_uri and switch to US-stock mode.
    
    Note
    
    If Qlib fails to connect redis via redis\_host and redis\_port, cache mechanism will not be used! Please refer to [Cache](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/latest/component/data.html#cache)
     for details.
    
*   exp\_manager
    
    Type: dict, optional parameter, the setting of experiment manager to be used in qlib. Users can specify an experiment manager class, as well as the tracking URI for all the experiments. However, please be aware that we only support input of a dictionary in the following style for exp\_manager. For more information about exp\_manager, users can refer to [Recorder: Experiment Management](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/latest/component/recorder.html)
    .
    
    \# For example, if you want to set your tracking\_uri to a <specific folder>, you can initialize qlib below
    qlib.init(provider\_uri\=provider\_uri, region\=REG\_CN, exp\_manager\= {
        "class": "MLflowExpManager",
        "module\_path": "qlib.workflow.expm",
        "kwargs": {
            "uri": "python\_execution\_path/mlruns",
            "default\_exp\_name": "Experiment",
        }
    })
    
*   mongo
    
    Type: dict, optional parameter, the setting of [MongoDB](https://www.mongodb.com/)
     which will be used in some features such as [Task Management](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/latest/advanced/task_management.html)
    , with high performance and clustered processing. Users need to follow the steps in [installation](https://www.mongodb.com/try/download/community)
     to install MongoDB firstly and then access it via a URI. Users can access mongodb with credential by setting “task\_url” to a string like “mongodb://%s:%s@%s” % (user, pwd, host + “:” + port).
    
    \# For example, you can initialize qlib below
    qlib.init(provider\_uri\=provider\_uri, region\=REG\_CN, mongo\={
        "task\_url": "mongodb://localhost:27017/",  \# your mongo url
        "task\_db\_name": "rolling\_db", \# the database name of Task Management
    })
    
*   logging\_level
    
    The logging level for the system.
    
*   kernels
    
    The number of processes used when calculating features in Qlib’s expression engine. It is very helpful to set it to 1 when you are debuggin an expression calculating exception

---

# Quick Start — QLib 0.9.8.dev11 documentation

*   [](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/latest/index.html)
    
*   Quick Start
*   [View page source](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/latest/_sources/introduction/quick.rst.txt)
    

* * *

Quick Start[](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/latest/introduction/quick.html#quick-start "Permalink to this heading")

======================================================================================================================

Introduction[](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/latest/introduction/quick.html#introduction "Permalink to this heading")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This `Quick Start` guide tries to demonstrate

*   It’s very easy to build a complete Quant research workflow and try users’ ideas with `Qlib`.
    
*   Though with public data and simple models, machine learning technologies work very well in practical Quant investment.
    

Installation[](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/latest/introduction/quick.html#installation "Permalink to this heading")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Users can easily install `Qlib` according to the following steps:

*   Before installing `Qlib` from source, users need to install some dependencies:
    
    > pip install numpy
    > pip install \--upgrade  cython
    
*   Clone the repository and install `Qlib`
    
    > git clone https://github.com/microsoft/qlib.git && cd qlib
    > python setup.py install
    

To known more about installation, please refer to [Qlib Installation](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/latest/start/installation.html)
.

Prepare Data[](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/latest/introduction/quick.html#prepare-data "Permalink to this heading")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Load and prepare data by running the following code:

python scripts/get\_data.py qlib\_data \--target\_dir ~/.qlib/qlib\_data/cn\_data \--region cn

This dataset is created by public data collected by crawler scripts in `scripts/data_collector/`, which have been released in the same repository. Users could create the same dataset with it.

To known more about prepare data, please refer to [Data Preparation](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/latest/component/data.html#data-preparation)
.

Auto Quant Research Workflow[](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/latest/introduction/quick.html#auto-quant-research-workflow "Permalink to this heading")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

`Qlib` provides a tool named `qrun` to run the whole workflow automatically (including building dataset, training models, backtest and evaluation). Users can start an auto quant research workflow and have a graphical reports analysis according to the following steps:

*   Quant Research Workflow:
    
    *   Run `qrun` with a config file of the LightGBM model workflow\_config\_lightgbm.yaml as following.
        
        > cd examples  \# Avoid running program under the directory contains \`qlib\`
        > qrun benchmarks/LightGBM/workflow\_config\_lightgbm.yaml
        
    *   Workflow result
        
        The result of `qrun` is as follows, which is also the typical result of `Forecast model(alpha)`. Please refer to [Intraday Trading](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/latest/component/backtest.html)
        . for more details about the result.
        
                                                          risk
        excess\_return\_without\_cost mean               0.000605
                                   std                0.005481
                                   annualized\_return  0.152373
                                   information\_ratio  1.751319
                                   max\_drawdown      \-0.059055
        excess\_return\_with\_cost    mean               0.000410
                                   std                0.005478
                                   annualized\_return  0.103265
                                   information\_ratio  1.187411
                                   max\_drawdown      \-0.075024
        
    
    To know more about workflow and qrun, please refer to [Workflow: Workflow Management](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/latest/component/workflow.html)
    .
    
*   Graphical Reports Analysis:
    
    *   Run `examples/workflow_by_code.ipynb` with jupyter notebook
        
        Users can have portfolio analysis or prediction score (model prediction) analysis by run `examples/workflow_by_code.ipynb`.
        
    *   Graphical Reports
        
        Users can get graphical reports about the analysis, please refer to [Analysis: Evaluation & Results Analysis](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/latest/component/report.html)
         for more details.
        
    

Custom Model Integration[](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/latest/introduction/quick.html#custom-model-integration "Permalink to this heading")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

`Qlib` provides a batch of models (such as `lightGBM` and `MLP` models) as examples of `Forecast Model`. In addition to the default model, users can integrate their own custom models into `Qlib`. If users are interested in the custom model, please refer to [Custom Model Integration](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/latest/start/integration.html)
.

---

# Data Retrieval — QLib 0.9.8.dev11 documentation

*   [](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/latest/index.html)
    
*   Data Retrieval
*   [View page source](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/latest/_sources/start/getdata.rst.txt)
    

* * *

Data Retrieval[](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/latest/start/getdata.html#data-retrieval "Permalink to this heading")

=======================================================================================================================

Introduction[](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/latest/start/getdata.html#introduction "Permalink to this heading")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Users can get stock data with `Qlib`. The following examples demonstrate the basic user interface.

Examples[](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/latest/start/getdata.html#examples "Permalink to this heading")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

`QLib` Initialization:

Note

In order to get the data, users need to initialize `Qlib` with qlib.init first. Please refer to [initialization](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/latest/start/initialization.html)
.

If users followed steps in [initialization](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/latest/start/initialization.html)
 and downloaded the data, they should use the following code to initialize qlib

\>> import qlib
\>> qlib.init(provider\_uri\='~/.qlib/qlib\_data/cn\_data')

Load trading calendar with given time range and frequency:

\>> from qlib.data import D
\>> D.calendar(start\_time\='2010-01-01', end\_time\='2017-12-31', freq\='day')\[:2\]
\[Timestamp('2010-01-04 00:00:00'), Timestamp('2010-01-05 00:00:00')\]

Parse a given market name into a stock pool config:

\>> from qlib.data import D
\>> D.instruments(market\='all')
{'market': 'all', 'filter\_pipe': \[\]}

Load instruments of certain stock pool in the given time range:

\>> from qlib.data import D
\>> instruments \= D.instruments(market\='csi300')
\>> D.list\_instruments(instruments\=instruments, start\_time\='2010-01-01', end\_time\='2017-12-31', as\_list\=True)\[:6\]
\['SH600036', 'SH600110', 'SH600087', 'SH600900', 'SH600089', 'SZ000912'\]

Load dynamic instruments from a base market according to a name filter

\>> from qlib.data import D
\>> from qlib.data.filter import NameDFilter
\>> nameDFilter \= NameDFilter(name\_rule\_re\='SH\[0-9\]{4}55')
\>> instruments \= D.instruments(market\='csi300', filter\_pipe\=\[nameDFilter\])
\>> D.list\_instruments(instruments\=instruments, start\_time\='2015-01-01', end\_time\='2016-02-15', as\_list\=True)
\['SH600655', 'SH601555'\]

Load dynamic instruments from a base market according to an expression filter

\>> from qlib.data import D
\>> from qlib.data.filter import ExpressionDFilter
\>> expressionDFilter \= ExpressionDFilter(rule\_expression\='$close>2000')
\>> instruments \= D.instruments(market\='csi300', filter\_pipe\=\[expressionDFilter\])
\>> D.list\_instruments(instruments\=instruments, start\_time\='2015-01-01', end\_time\='2016-02-15', as\_list\=True)
\['SZ000651', 'SZ000002', 'SH600655', 'SH600570'\]

For more details about filter, please refer [Filter API](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/latest/component/data.html)
.

Load features of certain instruments in a given time range:

\>> from qlib.data import D
\>> instruments \= \['SH600000'\]
\>> fields \= \['$close', '$volume', 'Ref($close, 1)', 'Mean($close, 3)', '$high-$low'\]
\>> D.features(instruments, fields, start\_time\='2010-01-01', end\_time\='2017-12-31', freq\='day').head().to\_string()
'                           $close     $volume  Ref($close, 1)  Mean($close, 3)  $high-$low
... instrument  datetime
... SH600000    2010\-01\-04  86.778313  16162960.0       88.825928        88.061483    2.907631
...             2010\-01\-05  87.433578  28117442.0       86.778313        87.679273    3.235252
...             2010\-01\-06  85.713585  23632884.0       87.433578        86.641825    1.720009
...             2010\-01\-07  83.788803  20813402.0       85.713585        85.645322    3.030487
...             2010\-01\-08  84.730675  16044853.0       83.788803        84.744354    2.047623'

Load features of certain stock pool in a given time range:

Note

With cache enabled, the qlib data server will cache data all the time for the requested stock pool and fields, it may take longer to process the request for the first time than that without cache. But after the first time, requests with the same stock pool and fields will hit the cache and be processed faster even the requested time period changes.

\>> from qlib.data import D
\>> from qlib.data.filter import NameDFilter, ExpressionDFilter
\>> nameDFilter \= NameDFilter(name\_rule\_re\='SH\[0-9\]{4}55')
\>> expressionDFilter \= ExpressionDFilter(rule\_expression\='$close>Ref($close,1)')
\>> instruments \= D.instruments(market\='csi300', filter\_pipe\=\[nameDFilter, expressionDFilter\])
\>> fields \= \['$close', '$volume', 'Ref($close, 1)', 'Mean($close, 3)', '$high-$low'\]
\>> D.features(instruments, fields, start\_time\='2010-01-01', end\_time\='2017-12-31', freq\='day').head().to\_string()
'                              $close        $volume  Ref($close, 1)  Mean($close, 3)  $high-$low
... instrument  datetime
... SH600655    2010\-01\-04  2699.567383  158193.328125     2619.070312      2626.097738  124.580566
...             2010\-01\-08  2612.359619   77501.406250     2584.567627      2623.220133   83.373047
...             2010\-01\-11  2712.982422  160852.390625     2612.359619      2636.636556  146.621582
...             2010\-01\-12  2788.688232  164587.937500     2712.982422      2704.676758  128.413818
...             2010\-01\-13  2790.604004  145460.453125     2788.688232      2764.091553  128.413818'

For more details about features, please refer [Feature API](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/latest/component/data.html)
.

Note

When calling D.features() at the client, use parameter disk\_cache=0 to skip dataset cache, use disk\_cache=1 to generate and use dataset cache. In addition, when calling at the server, users can use disk\_cache=2 to update the dataset cache.

When you are building complicated expressions, implementing all the expressions in a single string may not be easy. For example, it looks quite long and complicated:

\>> from qlib.data import D
\>> data \= D.features(\["sh600519"\], \["(($high / $close) + ($open / $close)) \* (($high / $close) + ($open / $close)) / (($high / $close) + ($open / $close))"\], start\_time\="20200101")

But using string is not the only way to implement the expression. You can also implement expression by code. Here is an example which does the same thing as above examples.

\>> from qlib.data.ops import \*
\>> f1 \= Feature("high") / Feature("close")
\>> f2 \= Feature("open") / Feature("close")
\>> f3 \= f1 + f2
\>> f4 \= f3 \* f3 / f3

\>> data \= D.features(\["sh600519"\], \[f4\], start\_time\="20200101")
\>> data.head()

API[](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/latest/start/getdata.html#api "Permalink to this heading")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

To know more about how to use the Data, go to API Reference: [Data API](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/latest/reference/api.html#data)

---

# Design of Nested Decision Execution Framework for High-Frequency Trading — QLib 0.9.8.dev11 documentation

*   [](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/latest/index.html)
    
*   Design of Nested Decision Execution Framework for High-Frequency Trading
*   [View page source](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/latest/_sources/component/highfreq.rst.txt)
    

* * *

Design of Nested Decision Execution Framework for High-Frequency Trading[](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/latest/component/highfreq.html#design-of-nested-decision-execution-framework-for-high-frequency-trading "Permalink to this heading")

================================================================================================================================================================================================================================================

Introduction[](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/latest/component/highfreq.html#introduction "Permalink to this heading")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Daily trading (e.g. portfolio management) and intraday trading (e.g. orders execution) are two hot topics in Quant investment and are usually studied separately.

To get the join trading performance of daily and intraday trading, they must interact with each other and run backtest jointly. In order to support the joint backtest strategies at multiple levels, a corresponding framework is required. None of the publicly available high-frequency trading frameworks considers multi-level joint trading, which makes the backtesting aforementioned inaccurate.

Besides backtesting, the optimization of strategies from different levels is not standalone and can be affected by each other. For example, the best portfolio management strategy may change with the performance of order executions(e.g. a portfolio with higher turnover may become a better choice when we improve the order execution strategies). To achieve overall good performance, it is necessary to consider the interaction of strategies at a different levels.

Therefore, building a new framework for trading on multiple levels becomes necessary to solve the various problems mentioned above, for which we designed a nested decision execution framework that considers the interaction of strategies.

![../_images/framework.svg](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/latest/_images/framework.svg)

The design of the framework is shown in the yellow part in the middle of the figure above. Each level consists of `Trading Agent` and `Execution Env`. `Trading Agent` has its own data processing module (`Information Extractor`), forecasting module (`Forecast Model`) and decision generator (`Decision Generator`). The trading algorithm generates the decisions by the `Decision Generator` based on the forecast signals output by the `Forecast Module`, and the decisions generated by the trading algorithm are passed to the `Execution Env`, which returns the execution results.

The frequency of the trading algorithm, decision content and execution environment can be customized by users (e.g. intraday trading, daily-frequency trading, weekly-frequency trading), and the execution environment can be nested with finer-grained trading algorithm and execution environment inside (i.e. sub-workflow in the figure, e.g. daily-frequency orders can be turned into finer-grained decisions by splitting orders within the day). The flexibility of the nested decision execution framework makes it easy for users to explore the effects of combining different levels of trading strategies and break down the optimization barriers between different levels of the trading algorithm.

The optimization for the nested decision execution framework can be implemented with the support of [QlibRL](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/latest/component/rl/overall.html)
. To know more about how to use the QlibRL, go to API Reference: [RL API](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/latest/reference/api.html#rl)
.

Example[](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/latest/component/highfreq.html#example "Permalink to this heading")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

An example of a nested decision execution framework for high-frequency can be found [here](https://github.com/microsoft/qlib/blob/main/examples/nested_decision_execution/workflow.py)
.

Besides, the above examples, here are some other related works about high-frequency trading in Qlib.

*   [Prediction with high-frequency data](https://github.com/microsoft/qlib/tree/main/examples/highfreq#benchmarks-performance-predicting-the-price-trend-in-high-frequency-data)
    
*   [Examples](https://github.com/microsoft/qlib/blob/main/examples/orderbook_data/)
     to extract features from high-frequency data without fixed frequency.
    
*   [A paper](https://github.com/microsoft/qlib/tree/high-freq-execution#high-frequency-execution)
     for high-frequency trading.

---

# Custom Model Integration — QLib 0.9.8.dev11 documentation

*   [](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/latest/index.html)
    
*   Custom Model Integration
*   [View page source](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/latest/_sources/start/integration.rst.txt)
    

* * *

Custom Model Integration[](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/latest/start/integration.html#custom-model-integration "Permalink to this heading")

===============================================================================================================================================

Introduction[](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/latest/start/integration.html#introduction "Permalink to this heading")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

`Qlib`’s Model Zoo includes models such as `LightGBM`, `MLP`, `LSTM`, etc.. These models are examples of `Forecast Model`. In addition to the default models `Qlib` provide, users can integrate their own custom models into `Qlib`.

Users can integrate their own custom models according to the following steps.

*   Define a custom model class, which should be a subclass of the [qlib.model.base.Model](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/latest/reference/api.html#module-qlib.model.base)
    .
    
*   Write a configuration file that describes the path and parameters of the custom model.
    
*   Test the custom model.
    

Custom Model Class[](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/latest/start/integration.html#custom-model-class "Permalink to this heading")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Custom models need to inherit [qlib.model.base.Model](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/latest/reference/api.html#module-qlib.model.base)
 and override the methods in it.

*   Override the \_\_init\_\_ method
    
    *   `Qlib` passes the initialized parameters to the \_\_init\_\_ method.
        
    *   The hyperparameters of model in the configuration must be consistent with those defined in the \_\_init\_\_ method.
        
    *   Code Example: In the following example, the hyperparameters of model in the configuration file should contain parameters such as loss:mse.
        
        > def \_\_init\_\_(self, loss\='mse', \*\*kwargs):
        >     if loss not in {'mse', 'binary'}:
        >         raise NotImplementedError
        >     self.\_scorer \= mean\_squared\_error if loss \== 'mse' else roc\_auc\_score
        >     self.\_params.update(objective\=loss, \*\*kwargs)
        >     self.\_model \= None
        
    
*   Override the fit method
    
    *   `Qlib` calls the fit method to train the model.
        
    *   The parameters must include training feature dataset, which is designed in the interface.
        
    *   The parameters could include some optional parameters with default values, such as num\_boost\_round = 1000 for GBDT.
        
    *   Code Example: In the following example, num\_boost\_round = 1000 is an optional parameter.
        
        > def fit(self, dataset: DatasetH, num\_boost\_round \= 1000, \*\*kwargs):
        > 
        >     \# prepare dataset for lgb training and evaluation
        >     df\_train, df\_valid \= dataset.prepare(
        >         \["train", "valid"\], col\_set\=\["feature", "label"\], data\_key\=DataHandlerLP.DK\_L
        >     )
        >     x\_train, y\_train \= df\_train\["feature"\], df\_train\["label"\]
        >     x\_valid, y\_valid \= df\_valid\["feature"\], df\_valid\["label"\]
        > 
        >     \# Lightgbm need 1D array as its label
        >     if y\_train.values.ndim \== 2 and y\_train.values.shape\[1\] \== 1:
        >         y\_train, y\_valid \= np.squeeze(y\_train.values), np.squeeze(y\_valid.values)
        >     else:
        >         raise ValueError("LightGBM doesn't support multi-label training")
        > 
        >     dtrain \= lgb.Dataset(x\_train.values, label\=y\_train)
        >     dvalid \= lgb.Dataset(x\_valid.values, label\=y\_valid)
        > 
        >     \# fit the model
        >     self.model \= lgb.train(
        >         self.params,
        >         dtrain,
        >         num\_boost\_round\=num\_boost\_round,
        >         valid\_sets\=\[dtrain, dvalid\],
        >         valid\_names\=\["train", "valid"\],
        >         early\_stopping\_rounds\=early\_stopping\_rounds,
        >         verbose\_eval\=verbose\_eval,
        >         evals\_result\=evals\_result,
        >         \*\*kwargs
        >     )
        
    
*   Override the predict method
    
    *   The parameters must include the parameter dataset, which will be used to get the test dataset.
        
    *   Return the prediction score.
        
    *   Please refer to [Model API](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/latest/reference/api.html#module-qlib.model.base)
         for the parameter types of the fit method.
        
    *   Code Example: In the following example, users need to use LightGBM to predict the label(such as preds) of test data x\_test and return it.
        
        > def predict(self, dataset: DatasetH, \*\*kwargs)\-> pandas.Series:
        >     if self.model is None:
        >         raise ValueError("model is not fitted yet!")
        >     x\_test \= dataset.prepare("test", col\_set\="feature", data\_key\=DataHandlerLP.DK\_I)
        >     return pd.Series(self.model.predict(x\_test.values), index\=x\_test.index)
        
    
*   Override the finetune method (Optional)
    
    *   This method is optional to the users. When users want to use this method on their own models, they should inherit the `ModelFT` base class, which includes the interface of finetune.
        
    *   The parameters must include the parameter dataset.
        
    *   Code Example: In the following example, users will use LightGBM as the model and finetune it.
        
        > def finetune(self, dataset: DatasetH, num\_boost\_round\=10, verbose\_eval\=20):
        >     \# Based on existing model and finetune by train more rounds
        >     dtrain, \_ \= self.\_prepare\_data(dataset)
        >     self.model \= lgb.train(
        >         self.params,
        >         dtrain,
        >         num\_boost\_round\=num\_boost\_round,
        >         init\_model\=self.model,
        >         valid\_sets\=\[dtrain\],
        >         valid\_names\=\["train"\],
        >         verbose\_eval\=verbose\_eval,
        >     )
        
    

Configuration File[](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/latest/start/integration.html#configuration-file "Permalink to this heading")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The configuration file is described in detail in the [Workflow](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/latest/component/workflow.html#complete-example)
 document. In order to integrate the custom model into `Qlib`, users need to modify the “model” field in the configuration file. The configuration describes which models to use and how we can initialize it.

*   Example: The following example describes the model field of configuration file about the custom lightgbm model mentioned above, where module\_path is the module path, class is the class name, and args is the hyperparameter passed into the \_\_init\_\_ method. All parameters in the field is passed to self.\_params by \*\*kwargs in \_\_init\_\_ except loss = mse.
    
    > model:
    >     class: LGBModel
    >     module\_path: qlib.contrib.model.gbdt
    >     args:
    >         loss: mse
    >         colsample\_bytree: 0.8879
    >         learning\_rate: 0.0421
    >         subsample: 0.8789
    >         lambda\_l1: 205.6999
    >         lambda\_l2: 580.9768
    >         max\_depth: 8
    >         num\_leaves: 210
    >         num\_threads: 20
    

Users could find configuration file of the baselines of the `Model` in `examples/benchmarks`. All the configurations of different models are listed under the corresponding model folder.

Model Testing[](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/latest/start/integration.html#model-testing "Permalink to this heading")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Assuming that the configuration file is `examples/benchmarks/LightGBM/workflow_config_lightgbm.yaml`, users can run the following command to test the custom model:

cd examples  \# Avoid running program under the directory contains \`qlib\`
qrun benchmarks/LightGBM/workflow\_config\_lightgbm.yaml

Note

`qrun` is a built-in command of `Qlib`.

Also, `Model` can also be tested as a single module. An example has been given in `examples/workflow_by_code.ipynb`.

Reference[](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/latest/start/integration.html#reference "Permalink to this heading")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

To know more about `Forecast Model`, please refer to [Forecast Model: Model Training & Prediction](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/latest/component/model.html)
 and [Model API](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/latest/reference/api.html#module-qlib.model.base)
.

---

# Guidance — QLib 0.9.8.dev11 documentation

*   [](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/latest/index.html)
    
*   [Reinforcement Learning in Quantitative Trading](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/latest/component/rl/toctree.html)
    
*   Guidance
*   [View page source](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/latest/_sources/component/rl/guidance.rst.txt)
    

* * *

Guidance[](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/latest/component/rl/guidance.html#guidance "Permalink to this heading")

===================================================================================================================

QlibRL can help users quickly get started and conveniently implement quantitative strategies based on reinforcement learning(RL) algorithms. For different user groups, we recommend the following guidance to use QlibRL.

Beginners to Reinforcement Learning Algorithms[](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/latest/component/rl/guidance.html#beginners-to-reinforcement-learning-algorithms "Permalink to this heading")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Whether you are a quantitative researcher who wants to understand what RL can do in trading or a learner who wants to get started with RL algorithms in trading scenarios, if you have limited knowledge of RL and want to shield various detailed settings to quickly get started with RL algorithms, we recommend the following sequence to learn qlibrl:

*   Learn the fundamentals of RL in [part1](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/latest/component/rl/overall.html#reinforcement-learning)
    .
    
*   Understand the trading scenarios where RL methods can be applied in [part2](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/latest/component/rl/overall.html#potential-application-scenarios-in-quantitative-trading)
    .
    
*   Run the examples in [part3](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/latest/component/rl/quickstart.html)
     to solve trading problems using RL.
    
*   If you want to further explore QlibRL and make some customizations, you need to first understand the framework of QlibRL in [part4](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/latest/component/rl/framework.html)
     and rewrite specific components according to your needs.
    

Reinforcement Learning Algorithm Researcher[](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/latest/component/rl/guidance.html#reinforcement-learning-algorithm-researcher "Permalink to this heading")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

If you are already familiar with existing RL algorithms and dedicated to researching RL algorithms but lack domain knowledge in the financial field, and you want to validate the effectiveness of your algorithms in financial trading scenarios, we recommend the following steps to get started with QlibRL:

*   Understand the trading scenarios where RL methods can be applied in [part2](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/latest/component/rl/overall.html#potential-application-scenarios-in-quantitative-trading)
    .
    
*   Choose an RL application scenario (currently, QlibRL has implemented two scenario examples: order execution and algorithmic trading). Run the example in [part3](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/latest/component/rl/quickstart.html)
     to get it working.
    
*   Modify the [policy](https://github.com/microsoft/qlib/blob/main/qlib/rl/order_execution/policy.py)
     part to incorporate your own RL algorithm.
    

Quantitative Researcher[](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/latest/component/rl/guidance.html#quantitative-researcher "Permalink to this heading")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

If you have a certain level of financial domain knowledge and coding skills, and you want to explore the application of RL algorithms in the investment field, we recommend the following steps to explore QlibRL:

*   Learn the fundamentals of RL in [part1](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/latest/component/rl/overall.html#reinforcement-learning)
    .
    
*   Understand the trading scenarios where RL methods can be applied in [part2](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/latest/component/rl/overall.html#potential-application-scenarios-in-quantitative-trading)
    .
    
*   Run the examples in [part3](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/latest/component/rl/quickstart.html)
     to solve trading problems using RL.
    
*   Understand the framework of QlibRL in [part4](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/latest/component/rl/framework.html)
    .
    
*   Choose a suitable RL algorithm based on the characteristics of the problem you want to solve (currently, QlibRL supports PPO and DQN algorithms based on tianshou).
    
*   Design the MDP (Markov Decision Process) process based on market trading rules and the problem you want to solve. Refer to the example in order execution and make corresponding modifications to the following modules: [State](https://github.com/microsoft/qlib/blob/main/qlib/rl/order_execution/state.py#L70)
    , [Metrics](https://github.com/microsoft/qlib/blob/main/qlib/rl/order_execution/state.py#L18)
    , [ActionInterpreter](https://github.com/microsoft/qlib/blob/main/qlib/rl/order_execution/interpreter.py#L199)
    , [StateInterpreter](https://github.com/microsoft/qlib/blob/main/qlib/rl/order_execution/interpreter.py#L68)
    , [Reward](https://github.com/microsoft/qlib/blob/main/qlib/rl/order_execution/reward.py)
    , [Observation](https://github.com/microsoft/qlib/blob/main/qlib/rl/order_execution/interpreter.py#L44)
    , [Simulator](https://github.com/microsoft/qlib/blob/main/qlib/rl/order_execution/simulator_simple.py)
    .

---

# Reinforcement Learning in Quantitative Trading — QLib 0.9.8.dev11 documentation

*   [](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/latest/index.html)
    
*   [Reinforcement Learning in Quantitative Trading](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/latest/component/rl/toctree.html)
    
*   Reinforcement Learning in Quantitative Trading
*   [View page source](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/latest/_sources/component/rl/overall.rst.txt)
    

* * *

Reinforcement Learning in Quantitative Trading[](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/latest/component/rl/overall.html#reinforcement-learning-in-quantitative-trading "Permalink to this heading")

==============================================================================================================================================================================================

Reinforcement Learning[](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/latest/component/rl/overall.html#reinforcement-learning "Permalink to this heading")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Different from supervised learning tasks such as classification tasks and regression tasks. Another important paradigm in machine learning is Reinforcement Learning(RL), which attempts to optimize an accumulative numerical reward signal by directly interacting with the environment under a few assumptions such as Markov Decision Process(MDP).

As demonstrated in the following figure, an RL system consists of four elements, 1)the agent 2) the environment the agent interacts with 3) the policy that the agent follows to take actions on the environment and 4)the reward signal from the environment to the agent. In general, the agent can perceive and interpret its environment, take actions and learn through reward, to seek long-term and maximum overall reward to achieve an optimal solution.

[![../../_images/RL_framework.png](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/latest/_images/RL_framework.png)](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/latest/_images/RL_framework.png)

RL attempts to learn to produce actions by trial and error. By sampling actions and then observing which one leads to our desired outcome, a policy is obtained to generate optimal actions. In contrast to supervised learning, RL learns this not from a label but from a time-delayed label called a reward. This scalar value lets us know whether the current outcome is good or bad. In a word, the target of RL is to take actions to maximize reward.

The Qlib Reinforcement Learning toolkit (QlibRL) is an RL platform for quantitative investment, which provides support to implement the RL algorithms in Qlib.

Potential Application Scenarios in Quantitative Trading[](https://qlib.readthedocs.io/en/latest/component/rl/overall.html#potential-application-scenarios-in-quantitative-trading "Permalink to this heading")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

RL methods have demonstrated remarkable achievements in various applications, including game playing, resource allocation, recommendation systems, marketing, and advertising. In the context of investment, which involves continuous decision-making, let’s consider the example of the stock market. Investors strive to optimize their investment returns by effectively managing their positions and stock holdings through various buying and selling behaviors. Furthermore, investors carefully evaluate market conditions and stock-specific information before making each buying or selling decision. From an investor’s perspective, this process can be viewed as a continuous decision-making process driven by interactions with the market. RL algorithms offer a promising approach to tackle such challenges. Here are several scenarios where RL holds potential for application in quantitative investment.
